title: Open Source Alpha: Daily AI Market Structure Analysis (BTC, NVDA, GOLD)
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi everyone, as a developer building herculmarket.com, I’ve realized that the hardest part of the trading day is filtering the noise at the US Open. To fix this, I’m starting a daily technical briefing powered by my AI structure analyzer, posted every day at 15:30 CET (US Opening).

Why follow these daily updates:

Pure Structure: No news hype. Just AI-detected liquidity sweeps and Order Blocks on high-volume assets like BTC, NVIDIA, and Gold.

Engineering Rigor: I’ll be sharing the cold data that my terminal uses to validate entries, like the 2.75 RR trade that bagged me 150 profit on Gold recently.

Data Integrity: Every analysis is backed by my PostgreSQL database, tracking real-time performance and XIRR to ensure we stay objective.

I’m doing this to stress-test the analyzer and get feedback from the community. If you want to access the full dashboard and track your own performance with the same stack, the beta is now live at herculmarket.com.

I’ll see you tomorrow at 15:30 for the first US Open breakdown. What other assets should the AI focus on? 📈💻

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body:
Appreciate the focus on structure over headlines — especially around the US open when things get noisy fast.

A couple of questions that might help people evaluate the output:

- How are you defining liquidity sweeps programmatically (wick penetration + close back inside range, volume threshold, etc.)?
- Are the order blocks derived purely from displacement + imbalance logic, or are you incorporating volume profile / delta?
- Do you publish historical signals so people can see how the model behaved in different regimes (trend vs chop)?

If you’re aiming for “engineering rigor,” having a small public track record sample (even just annotated charts with timestamps vs outcome) would go a long way toward building trust.

Looking forward to seeing how the BTC/NVDA/GOLD structure compares day to day — especially whether the model adapts well when volatility compresses.

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Thanks for the detailed questions—this is exactly the kind of technical feedback I’m looking for to refine the terminal.

Liquidity Sweeps: The logic currently triggers on wick penetration of a significant swing high/low followed by a candle body close back within the prior range on the M15 timeframe.

Order Blocks: These are primarily derived from displacement + FVG (Fair Value Gap) imbalance logic. I’m currently exploring the integration of volume delta to filter out low-conviction blocks, but the current version stays pure price action for simplicity.

Public Track Record: I agree that trust is built on data. My dashboard is built on Supabase/PostgreSQL specifically to log every trade with timestamps and outcomes, providing a transparent look at the strategy’s expectancy.

Market Adaptation: The model focuses on high-volume assets like BTC, NVDA, and Gold because they tend to respect structure even when volatility compresses, like during my recent 2.75 RR trade on Gold.

The goal is to move all these metrics into a public "Performance Hub" on herculmarket.com so anyone can verify the model’s behavior across different regimes.

The beta is free if you want to see the charts and the logging logic in action
